proven: LIVE-PROVEN 2026-07-20, prefix 90777, import ProcessId 100007866630904 / ESS request 9765415 (fin_impl). Good lookup type RT_GOLD_90777 + codes G1,G2 reached FND_LOOKUP_TYPES_VL / FND_LOOKUP_VALUES_VL (over base _B/_TL). Bad code BAD1 (parent RT_NO_SUCH_TYPE_90777) rejected by the importer ('Parent row is missing in file FND_APP_STANDARD_LOOKUP.csv for row 3 ... Row will be skipped') and absent from base.
mechanism_note: Lookups was previously TABLED as UI-only. That was wrong: the FSM 'Setup Data Import from CSV file' REST path DOES support it. GET setupTaskCSVImports/FND_MANAGE_STANDARD_LOOKUPS returns ImportSupportedFlag=true, and (unlike GLCalendar) Standard Lookups is a PLAIN FLAT-CSV object, not 'External Loading' - the import ProcessLog reports 'Total Objects for External Loading: 0' and the CSVs load directly through the LookupWS service. This is the same non-UI mechanism GLCalendar found and UnitsOfMeasure proved; driven by harness/load_fsm_csv.py.

package_layout.note: Three files at the ROOT of the zip (NOT nested in a batch subzip - this is a flat-CSV object). Learned by exporting the live task (setupTaskCSVExports, ProcessType flipped EXPORT->IMPORT in the manifest).
members: ORA_FND_APP_STANDARD_LOOKUP_CODE.csv (the lookup CODES; first column is parent-qualified 'FND_APP_STANDARD_LOOKUP.LookupType')
ASM_SETUP_CSV_METADATA.xml (real, exported live from esew-dev28; ProcessType EXPORT->IMPORT)
package_layout.csv_format: COMMA-delimited, every field DOUBLE-QUOTED, CRLF line ends, header row present. Dates 'YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI:SS.FF'. (NOTE: this supersedes the earlier UI-doc assumption of pipe-delimited unquoted files.)

business_objects.min_required: LookupType, Meaning, ModuleId (40B3FA7250D19380E040449823C67A1A = the FND application on this pod, read from the live export), CustomizationLevel (U), RestAccessSecured (SECURE)

business_objects.role: lookup codes (FND_LOOKUP_VALUES_B/_TL), child of the type
business_objects.note: First column is the parent-qualified key 'FND_APP_STANDARD_LOOKUP.LookupType' - it links each code to its type. A code whose parent LookupType is not in the type CSV is skipped with 'Parent row is missing'.

good_bad_design.good: NEW lookup type RT_GOLD_${PREFIX} (meaning 'RT Gold Lookup ${PREFIX}') with two enabled codes G1, G2. Standalone reference data - no upstream dependency, no discovery (ModuleId for FND is read once from the live export).
good_bad_design.bad: A code row BAD1 whose parent LookupType = RT_NO_SUCH_TYPE_${PREFIX} is NOT present in the type CSV. The importer skips it ('Parent row is missing') so it never reaches FND_LOOKUP_VALUES - deterministic, pod-independent.

verify.note: The BIP relay data source (ApplicationDB_FSCM) is not granted the _B tables directly (ORA-00942), but IS granted the translated views FND_LOOKUP_TYPES_VL / FND_LOOKUP_VALUES_VL (which sit over _B + _TL). Verify through the _VL views.

base_read_types.sql: SELECT lookup_type AS LOOKUP_TYPE FROM fnd_lookup_types_vl WHERE lookup_type = 'RT_GOLD_${PREFIX}'

base_read_values.sql: SELECT lookup_type AS LOOKUP_TYPE, lookup_code AS LOOKUP_CODE FROM fnd_lookup_values_vl WHERE lookup_type = 'RT_GOLD_${PREFIX}' AND lookup_code IN ('G1','G2')

bad_absent_read.sql: SELECT lookup_type AS LOOKUP_TYPE, lookup_code AS LOOKUP_CODE FROM fnd_lookup_values_vl WHERE lookup_type = 'RT_NO_SUCH_TYPE_${PREFIX}'
bad_absent_read.expect: zero rows (parent LookupType absent -> code skipped, never reaches base)
